specification:
Type: workflow

Purpose: Help students determine what happens in different accident scenarios based on coverage types

Learning objective: **Applying** - Use knowledge of auto insurance coverage types to determine which coverage pays in specific scenarios

Visual style: Interactive decision tree with scenario cards

Structure:

Start: "Auto Accident or Damage Occurs"

Decision 1: "What type of incident?"
- Branch A: "You caused an accident" → Go to Decision 2A
- Branch B: "Another driver hit you" → Go to Decision 2B
- Branch C: "Non-collision damage (theft, weather, animal)" → Go to Decision 2C

Decision 2A: "You caused an accident"
- Your injuries/car: "Collision Coverage" (if you have it) + "MedPay" (if you have it)
- Other driver's injuries/car: "Your Liability Coverage"
- Hover text: "You're responsible for others' damages. Your liability pays for them, collision pays for your car."

Decision 2B: "Another driver hit you"
- Decision: "Do they have insurance?"
  - Yes → "Their Liability Coverage" pays for you
  - No → "Your Uninsured Motorist Coverage" (if you have it)
  - Insufficient → "Your Underinsured Motorist Coverage" (if you have it)
- Hover text: "If they can't pay, your UM/UIM coverage protects you"

Decision 2C: "Non-collision damage"
- "Comprehensive Coverage" (if you have it)
- Examples: Theft, vandalism, weather, hitting animal, glass damage
- Hover text: "Comprehensive covers almost everything except collisions and mechanical issues"

Example scenarios with outcomes:

Scenario 1: "You rear-end another car"
- Their damages: Your Liability Coverage pays
- Your car: Your Collision Coverage pays (minus deductible)
- Your injuries: Your MedPay or health insurance
- Result: Premium will likely increase

Scenario 2: "Deer runs into your car"
- Your car damages: Your Comprehensive Coverage pays (minus deductible)
- Note: This is comprehensive, NOT collision!
- Premium impact: Usually minimal or none

Scenario 3: "Hit by uninsured driver"
- Your damages: Your Uninsured Motorist Coverage pays
- Alternative: Your Collision Coverage + sue the driver
- Hover text: "This is why UM coverage is so important"

Scenario 4: "Car stolen"
- Your car value: Your Comprehensive Coverage pays
- Gap (if owed > value): Your Gap Insurance pays
- Hover text: "You'll receive actual cash value, not replacement cost"

Color coding:
- Blue: Your coverage pays
- Orange: Their coverage pays
- Red: No coverage/you pay out-of-pocket
- Green: Resolved favorably

Interactive features:
- Click scenario to see coverage breakdown
- Hover over coverage types for definitions
- Toggle "Show Coverage Recommendation" to see smart coverage advice
- Input your car's value and see if collision/comprehensive make sense

Implementation: HTML/CSS/JavaScript with interactive tree structure
Canvas size: 900x700px
